implementation.approach: CLI and UI exposure of P4.1-P4.6 features, plus physical acceptance matrix. No new authorization or transport behavior; presentation and validation only.

cli_onboarding.dllm_peer_status: new command calling GET /v1/peer-network/status. Renders all PeerDiagnostics fields in human-readable format with --json flag for raw output.
cli_onboarding.dllm_status_extended: added forwarding policy count, transport binding count with expiry warning (<24h), resource budget count, and ban list size to non-JSON output.
cli_onboarding.list_command_reformat: ListAccessRequests, ListAbuseReports, and AuditLog now print tables by default (node key/endpoint/age/note for access requests; reporter/subject/category/age/note for abuse reports; timestamp/actor/action/target/outcome for audit log). All three accept --json for raw output.
cli_onboarding.dllm_onboard: guided sequence: ensures node and transport identities exist, submits access request, polls owner endpoint for approval up to configurable timeout, prints next steps on success.

ui.pattern: follows existing section + fetch + mutate + refresh pattern. hexToBytes helper for pubkey inputs.

files_changed.crates/dllm-transport/src/peer.rs: added dht_hosting: bool field to PeerDiagnostics; populated from config.dht_hosting in initial diagnostics
files_changed.crates/dllm-cli/src/main.rs: added PeerStatus, Onboard command variants; extended Status output; reformatted ListAccessRequests/ListAbuseReports/AuditLog to table output with --json flag; added format_hex, format_age helpers
files_changed.web/index.html: added 8 new sections (peer network, transport bindings, forwarding policy, access requests, resource budgets, moderation, abuse reports, audit log) with JS rendering and mutation handlers

physical_validation.topology: 2 local daemons (kansas owner/bootstrap/forwarder on :7337/:7444, newyork forwarding member on :7338/:7445) with P2P enabled

verified.detail: Both kansas and newyork report peer ID, discovery mode, DHT hosting, forwarding eligibility, bootstrap/discovered counts, stream counters, error counters via dllm peer-status
Table output with node key, endpoint, age, note columns
Table output with reporter, subject, category, age, note columns
Table output with timestamp, actor, action, target, outcome columns
dht_hosting: true appears as 'server' in peer-status output

deferred: Full 24-scenario matrix requires stable 3-node topology (KS bootstrap, NY forwarder, CO edge behind NAT)
Laptop edge node deployment (binary built, state and keys prepared at /tmp/dllm-p47/colorado/)
HTTP API /v1/status timeout observed when daemon runs with P2P — pre-existing, not caused by P4.7 changes
physical_validation.setup_artifacts: /tmp/dllm-p47/ contains state, keys, and scripts for reproducibility

phase4_closeout.criteria_1: single binary install, join network, discover peers, authenticate, serve inference: IMPLEMENTED across P4.0-P4.6, CLI/UI exposed in P4.7
phase4_closeout.criteria_2: discovery records publish reachability only; owner-signed state is authoritative: PROVEN per-milestone, confirmed end-to-end by P4.7 documentation
phase4_closeout.criteria_3: bootstrap/discovery/NAT traversal/forwarding are ordinary dllmd capabilities: IMPLEMENTED, no dedicated infrastructure required
phase4_closeout.criteria_4: direct connection where possible, automatic forwarding otherwise per signed policy: IMPLEMENTED and observable via peer-status
phase4_closeout.criteria_5: status and diagnostics report discovered endpoint, path, forwarding member, failures: EXPOSED in CLI (peer-status, extended status) and UI (peer network section)

independence_invariants.no_new_api_routes: All CLI and UI changes consume existing P4.1-P4.6 routes; no new handlers added
independence_invariants.no_new_authorization: No changes to AuthView, membership, or transport authorization
independence_invariants.presentation_only: CLI formatting and UI rendering are observational; no state mutations added
